tasks.title: Achieve feature parity across all runners
evidence: Claude runner exposes unique flags like --verbose and --permission-mode that are not consistently mapped or available for other runners.
Gemini uses a hardcoded --approval-mode yolo which should be configurable or standardized.
Resume flags vary significantly across runners (resume vs -s vs --resume).
Output format selection is inconsistent (--json vs --format json vs --output-format stream-json).
Streaming consistency is not guaranteed when interleaved with non-JSON output, particularly in Codex and OpenCode.
plan: Explore the docs and command help menus for each of the supported runners to understand their capabilities and limitations as not all will be supported in the same way.
Audit all runner CLI capabilities for shared features such as verbose mode and approval/permission settings.
Incorporate shared flag support into the RunnerCommandBuilder proposed by RQ-0297.
Refactor run_with_streaming_json in process.rs to handle varied JSON output styles and interleaved logs consistently.
Expose standardized settings (e.g., verbose, approval_mode) in AgentConfig and propagate them to runners.
Verify parity using integration tests that simulate diverse runner CLI outputs and edge cases.
Update documentation to reflect the standardized runner interface.
notes: This task is a logical follow-up to the DRY refactoring in RQ-0297.
Improved parsing logic from RQ-0299 will be foundational for consistent streaming.
